description:
Context:
      You are a QA engineer who has just received three raw data files that were
      generated by the nightly regression pipeline.  
      • /home/user/raw_data/test_cases.list   – colon “:” separated  
      • /home/user/raw_data/statuses.log      – comma “,” separated  
      • /home/user/raw_data/durations.csv     – semicolon “;” separated  

      1)  test_cases.list  holds three columns in the exact order  
            a. Test-case-ID  
            b. Human-readable title  
            c. Environment name (env1, env2, …)

      2)  statuses.log  holds two columns in the exact order  
            a. Test-case-ID  
            b. Execution status (PASS | FAIL | SKIP)

      3)  durations.csv  holds two columns in the exact order  
            a. Test-case-ID  
            b. Duration in milliseconds (integer)

    Goal:
      Produce a consolidated, tab-separated report by cutting the appropriate
      columns from the three input files and pasting them side-by-side in the
      correct order.

    Target artefacts that the automated grader will inspect:

      • Directory  :  /home/user/final_reports
          – must be created if it does not exist.

      • Report file:  /home/user/final_reports/QA_Combined_Report.tsv  
          – must be UTF-8 text, Unix line endings  
          – first (header) line EXACTLY (tabs between the column names):  
                TestCaseID	Title	Environment	Status	Duration_ms  

          – starting on the second line, each line must contain exactly five
            tab-separated fields in the order shown in the header.  
          – Lines must appear sorted by TestCaseID in ascending order
            (the raw files are already in that order, you do not have to re-sort
             them, but do not reorder them either).  
          – The numeric duration value must appear as-is (no extra units,
            no quotes, no thousands separators).

      • Assembly log: /home/user/final_reports/assembly.log  
          – plain text, UTF-8, Unix line endings  
          – for every non-trivial shell command you issue, append a line
            containing the command preceded by an ISO-8601 timestamp.  
            Example log line format (including the square brackets):
              [2024-03-14T22:17:02+00:00] cut -d':' -f1,2 test_cases.list
          – The grader will only check that the file exists and contains at
            least three timestamped lines, so you are free to log as many
            or as few commands as you wish provided this minimum is met.

    Constraints & hints:

      • Do NOT edit the original source files in /home/user/raw_data.  
      • Use only standard POSIX command-line utilities that are available in a
        typical minimal Linux environment (cut, paste, printf, cat, etc.).  
      • You do not have root privileges; work entirely in your home directory.  
      • Remember that the output file must be TAB-separated, while each source
        file uses its own delimiter.  
      • Ensure all created files and directories are world-readable
        (the default umask should already guarantee this).  
      • After finishing, double-check that  
          $ file /home/user/final_reports/QA_Combined_Report.tsv  
        reports “ASCII text” or “UTF-8 Unicode text” and that the header line
        is present and correct.

    Success criteria:

      1) /home/user/final_reports exists.  
      2) /home/user/final_reports/QA_Combined_Report.tsv exists, is non-empty,
         contains the exact header, and has exactly the same number of data
         lines as appear in the source files.  
      3) Each data line shows the TestCaseID, Title, Environment, Status and
         Duration_ms fields resolved from the three input files and separated
         by TAB characters.  
      4) /home/user/final_reports/assembly.log exists and contains at least
         three well-formed timestamped log lines as described above.
truth:
Initial file system state (already present before the agent starts):

    Directory: /home/user/raw_data
      Files and their full contents:

      1. /home/user/raw_data/test_cases.list
         ------------------------------------
         TC001:UserLogin:env1
         TC002:UserLogout:env1
         TC003:PurchaseItem:env2
         TC004:CancelOrder:env2
         TC005:ApplyCoupon:env3

      2. /home/user/raw_data/statuses.log
         --------------------------------
         TC001,PASS
         TC002,FAIL
         TC003,PASS
         TC004,SKIP
         TC005,FAIL

      3. /home/user/raw_data/durations.csv
         ---------------------------------
         TC001;1023
         TC002;980
         TC003;2150
         TC004;1500
         TC005;1175

  Expected artefacts after successful completion:

    1. Directory:
       /home/user/final_reports      (permissions 755 or 775 acceptable)

    2. File: /home/user/final_reports/QA_Combined_Report.tsv
       Exact contents (including the trailing newline after every line):
         TestCaseID	Title	Environment	Status	Duration_ms
         TC001	UserLogin	env1	PASS	1023
         TC002	UserLogout	env1	FAIL	980
         TC003	PurchaseItem	env2	PASS	2150
         TC004	CancelOrder	env2	SKIP	1500
         TC005	ApplyCoupon	env3	FAIL	1175

    3. File: /home/user/final_reports/assembly.log
       – must exist, UTF-8 text, at least 3 lines matching:
         ^\[[0-9T:+-]+\] .+   (verified by regex)
       – the actual commands logged are not fixed and therefore are not shown
         here; only the presence and pattern are tested.
